steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: When two lines intersect at a point, they form four angles. The angles opposite each other are called vertical angles (or vertically opposite angles). From the figure, $r°$ and $s°$ are vertical angles.

steps.title: Apply the vertical angles theorem
steps.content:
The Vertical Angles Theorem states that vertical angles are congruent—they have equal measures. Since $r$ and $s$ are vertical angles:
$$s = r$$
Substituting $r = 61$: $$s = 61$$
That matches the expected result — $s = 61$.
